purpose: Overall execution plan for expanding curated Skills from discovery through reviewed release and final local Codex, agents, and cc Switch alignment. This is not approval, release inventory, runtime installation evidence, or local sync authorization.

steps.goal: Establish broad domain coverage and safe discovery inputs beyond software engineering.
actions: define collaboration domains
record starred and GitHub-wide discovery inputs
keep stars as weak ranking evidence
keep discovery read-only and non-approval
acceptanceCriteria: coverage registry validates broad digital work domains
discovery profile is read-only
candidate sources are named without approval or runtime eligibility
software-engineering-only framing is rejected
verificationStandards: GitHub discovery workflow does not commit, install, or approve candidates
candidate records state non-approval and non-runtime boundaries

steps.goal: Pin small, reviewable source batches and prepare them for license, provenance, security, portability, and overlap review.
actions: pin source repository revisions as full SHAs
record license posture and source metadata
record detected Skill paths and coverage hints
name review focus for each source
block runtime and release actions until review closes
acceptanceCriteria: each source has a full pinned revision
each source has a supported or explicitly review-blocking license posture
each source has detected Skill evidence and coverage hints
each source has a review focus
the next gate is license-provenance-security-portability-overlap-review
verificationStandards: source-intake batch blocks release manifest changes
source-intake batch blocks local Codex/agents/cc-switch sync

steps.goal: Review pinned candidates and create neutral, portable, non-duplicative adaptation decisions before any release change.
actions: review license and attribution requirements
review provenance and source stability
review executable commands, network calls, credentials, and file-system boundaries
review portability across agents and runtimes
resolve overlap with existing curated, official, runtime, and local capabilities
draft adaptations only when the source passes review
acceptanceCriteria: every candidate has adopt, merge, recipe-only, adapter-only, reference-only, or reject disposition
security-sensitive commands are removed, bounded, or rejected
agent-specific language is neutralized unless it is an explicit dependency boundary
duplicates are merged or rejected instead of added as parallel Skills
copyright and license constraints are preserved
verificationStandards: review evidence links to source pins
adaptation drafts are not in release-manifest.json
no candidate becomes executable routing target before approval
blockedActions: approved payload admission without owner approval

steps.goal: Admit only approved adapted payload, update topology and release inventory, and prove the repository remains internally consistent.
actions: apply approved adapted payload changes
update admissions, skills, capabilities, relations, conflicts, recipes, routing, and scenarios when needed
rebuild deterministic topology and release manifest
run full repository verification
record release decision and residual risks
acceptanceCriteria: all admitted payload is approved and source-attributed
release-manifest.json contains only approved curated payload
generated routing projections are deterministic
routing scenarios cover new or changed behavior
no official, runtime-owned, or built-in Skill body is vendored as curated payload

steps.goal: Prepare the approved curated release for downstream consumption without yet mutating local runtime directories.
actions: record the exact curated repository SHA
prepare consumer sync plan
classify shared portable Skills versus environment-specific or runtime-owned Skills
prepare backup and rollback checks
define local parity checks for Codex, agents, and cc Switch
acceptanceCriteria: approved repository SHA is pinned
sync plan distinguishes portable curated Skills from ecosystem-specific Skills
Codex-specific official or runtime-owned Skills are not claimed as curated payload
agents and cc Switch parity target is explicit
Codex local target accounts for Codex-specific system or runtime surfaces
verificationStandards: git status --short --branch is clean before sync preparation closes
release-manifest.json hash set is the sync input
backup and rollback commands are reviewed before local writes

steps.goal: Align local Codex, agents, and cc Switch Skill directories after approved release readiness and explicit local-write authorization.
actions: back up or snapshot owned local Skill directories
sync approved portable curated Skills to agents and cc Switch targets
sync appropriate shared curated Skills into Codex without overwriting Codex system, plugin, or runtime-owned surfaces
preserve user-owned local custom Skills unless the current transaction owns them
verify no unintended reparse, shortcut, stale, or duplicate directory remains
record final parity and residual exceptions
acceptanceCriteria: local write authorization is explicit for this stage
agents and cc Switch contain the same approved portable curated Skill set or documented exceptions
Codex contains the approved shared curated Skill set plus documented Codex-specific surfaces
no source-intake-only or draft candidate becomes installed
backup, rollback, parity, and final inventory evidence exist
stage closeout states complete, partial, blocked, needs-verification, needs-user-confirmation, or cannot-close
verificationStandards: approved release manifest hashes match synced curated payload where applicable
directory reparse or shortcut status is checked
git status for repository remains clean after local sync evidence is recorded
blockedActions: overwriting official or runtime-owned Codex surfaces
claiming global completion without residual exception review

stageCloseoutRule: Do not align local Codex, agents, or cc Switch Skill directories until the curated release is approved, consumer projection readiness is complete, and local-write authorization is explicit for the alignment stage.
